title: Pod Security, Security Contexts, Images, Admission, Secrets, and Multi-Tenancy · Kubernetes Operations Room
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working restricted workload and admission policy for pod security, security contexts, images, admission, secrets, and multi-tenancy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of restricted workload and admission policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.
Set non-root identity, groups, filesystem ownership, read-only root, capabilities, privilege escalation, seccomp, host namespaces, devices, and writable mounts from threat evidence.
Require immutable images, provenance, signatures, vulnerability policy, registry allowlists, architecture, pull credentials, and admission decisions with exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Security Standards Admission
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Security Standards Admission.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for apply privileged, baseline, and restricted pod security standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Pod Security Standards Admission, keep K8s Pod Security Standards Admission connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop Pod runs as root, is privileged, adds capabilities, mounts hostPath, permits privilege escalation, and bypasses a namespace policy exception. Enforce Pod Security, securityContext, seccomp, admission, exception ownership, and residual shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Apply Privileged, Baseline, and Restricted Pod Security Standards with enforce, audit, warn, version pinning, exemptions, namespace labels, and rollout evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense-12h6h1". Tailor its restricted-workload-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense, keep K8s Multitenancy Isolation Defense connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense-12h6h1
  - competency: k8s-multitenancy-isolation-defense
    artifact: restricted-workload-policy.yaml
    decision: multitenancy isolation defense
    environment: clean conformance cluster with only required add-ons installed
    fault-injection: rotate a projected token while the API dependency slows
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: 12h6h1
    change: |
      pod-security: restricted-v1.36
      runAsNonRoot: true
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities-drop: ALL
      seccompProfile: RuntimeDefault
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
      image: digest-and-signature-required
      admission: fail-closed-with-breakglass
    verify: kubectl auth can-i create pods -n intake && kubectl apply --server-side --dry-run=server -f restricted.yaml
    failure: Reject removed PodSecurityPolicy, namespace-only multi-tenancy, mutable images, privileged host access, broad secrets, or unavailable admission without a plan.
    repair: Enforce versioned Pod Security, minimize container and API authority, isolate network and storage, verify supply chain, and test break-glass recovery.
